questions.question: Define similar figures and explain with examples.
questions.answer: Similar figures are figures that have the same shape but not necessarily the same size. For example, all circles are similar and all equilateral triangles are similar.

questions.question: What are similar triangles? Explain the conditions for similarity.
questions.answer: Two triangles are said to be similar if their corresponding angles are equal and their corresponding sides are proportional.

questions.question: Explain why all equilateral triangles are similar.
questions.answer: All equilateral triangles have all angles equal to 60° and their corresponding sides are proportional. Hence, they are similar.

questions.question: State and explain the Basic Proportionality Theorem.
questions.answer: The Basic Proportionality Theorem states that if a line is drawn parallel to one side of a triangle, it divides the other two sides in the same ratio.

questions.question: Explain the converse of the Basic Proportionality Theorem.
questions.answer: If a line divides two sides of a triangle in the same ratio, then the line is parallel to the third side.

questions.question: Differentiate between congruent triangles and similar triangles.
questions.answer: Congruent triangles have the same shape and size, while similar triangles have the same shape but may differ in size.

questions.question: Explain the AAA similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If all corresponding angles of two triangles are equal, then the triangles are similar. This is known as the AAA similarity criterion.

questions.question: Explain the AA similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If two angles of one triangle are equal to two angles of another triangle, then the third angles are also equal, making the triangles similar.

questions.question: Explain the SSS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If the corresponding sides of two triangles are proportional, then the triangles are similar. This is called the SSS similarity criterion.

questions.question: Explain the SAS similarity criterion.
questions.answer: If one angle of a triangle is equal to one angle of another triangle and the sides including those angles are proportional, then the triangles are similar.

questions.question: What is meant by scale factor? Explain with an example.
questions.answer: Scale factor is the ratio of corresponding sides of similar triangles. For example, if sides are in the ratio 2:3, the scale factor is 2:3.

questions.question: Explain why all circles are similar.
questions.answer: All circles have the same shape regardless of their radii, so all circles are similar.

questions.question: Explain why a square and a rectangle are not similar.
questions.answer: Although their angles are equal, the corresponding sides of a square and a rectangle are not proportional.

questions.question: Explain the role of parallel lines in triangle similarity.
questions.answer: Parallel lines help establish proportional sides in triangles using the Basic Proportionality Theorem.

questions.question: Explain indirect measurement using similar triangles.
questions.answer: Indirect measurement uses similar triangles to find heights or distances that cannot be measured directly.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by corresponding sides and angles.
questions.answer: Corresponding sides and angles are those that occupy the same relative position in similar figures.

questions.question: Explain why corresponding angles are equal in similar triangles.
questions.answer: Similar triangles have the same shape, so their corresponding angles are equal.

questions.question: Explain why corresponding sides are proportional in similar triangles.
questions.answer: Similar triangles are scaled versions of each other, so their sides increase or decrease in the same ratio.

questions.question: Explain why all congruent triangles are similar.
questions.answer: Congruent triangles have equal angles and equal sides, satisfying all conditions of similarity.

questions.question: Explain why similar triangles need not be congruent.
questions.answer: Similar triangles may differ in size even though their shapes are the same.

questions.question: Explain the importance of triangle similarity in geometry.
questions.answer: Triangle similarity helps in solving problems related to heights, distances, and geometric proofs.

questions.question: Explain the use of similarity in map drawing.
questions.answer: Maps use similarity to represent large distances on a smaller scale accurately.

questions.question: Explain how triangle similarity helps in surveying.
questions.answer: Surveying uses similar triangles to calculate distances that cannot be measured directly.

questions.question: Explain why correct correspondence is necessary in similarity statements.
questions.answer: Correct correspondence ensures correct comparison of angles and sides between triangles.

questions.question: Explain why triangle similarity is considered a basic concept in geometry.
questions.answer: Triangle similarity forms the foundation for many geometric proofs and real-life applications.
